analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 (counterparty IDs): forward mid moves after prints, stratified by aggressor inference (price vs BBO), K in {5,20,100} tape rows, spread quantile (tight/mid/wide on traded symbol), burst flag, hour; pair-cell baseline means; buyer→seller graph; burst vs random-time extract controls; consecutive-trade two-hop triple counts; adverse aggressor summary.
used_to_decide: Tape days 1–3 per Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4. Horizon K = K rows forward in price CSV for same (day, symbol). See round4_phase1_complete for gate checklist.

round4_phase1_complete.horizon_definition: K \in {5,20,100} means K rows forward in prices_round_4_day_*.csv after the row matching (day, timestamp, symbol); forward mid change = mid(t+K) - mid(t).

bullets.conclusion: Strongest signal: **Mark 67 aggressive buys** on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** in **tight** spread_q show large positive mean forward mids (K=5: mean≈1.98, t≈12.7, n≈154; K=20 similar). **Mark 55 aggressive sells** extract at K=100 mid spread show **negative** mean fwd (≈-0.94, p≈0.015, n≈538). **Mark 22** sell_agg on **VEV_5300** tight+**burst** K=5: negative mean (≈-0.20, p≈0.001, n=160). Mark 01 buy_agg on VEV_5200 tight had n=0 in per-day slice (trades often inside spread → unk).

bullets.topic: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (pair × symbol × K × regime cell means)
bullets.conclusion: Residuals vs (buyer|seller, symbol, K, spread_q, burst) cell means are **~machine zero** everywhere in the top table — dominant structure is **explained by the coarse pair/regime cells**; no large stable second-order residual in this parameterization.

Graph matches prior sketch: **Mark 01→Mark 22** dominates (1339 prints). Consecutive-trade two-hop buyer→seller→(next seller) shows **Mark 14→Mark 38→Mark 14** and **Mark 38→Mark 14→Mark 38** oscillation (≈165 / 160) — **duopoly ping-pong**, not a one-way insider chain.

Multi-print timestamps (n≈774 burst events): mean **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** forward mid change is **higher** after bursts than random same-day controls for K=20 (burst mean ≈0.33 vs control ≈0.11; K=5 also higher). Mid-only; tradable PnL not tested here.

**Mark 38 buy_agg** and **Mark 55 sell_agg** have the **most negative** mean 20-tick same-symbol markouts among marks with n≥15; **Mark 67 buy_agg** most positive — consistent with long-extract pressure after Mark 67 lifts.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: After **multi-symbol burst** at a timestamp, **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** mid tends to drift **up** more than random controls over **K=20** tape rows (effect ≈0.22 mid ticks; n_bursts=774 vs n_ctrl=2321).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Mid not bid/ask; confirm with --match-trades worse sim before sizing.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 67** **aggressive buy** on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** when extract BBO is **tight** → large **positive** forward mids at K=5/20/100 (n≈154; t≥5).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Low-frequency actor vs Mark 55; watch overfit.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 55 aggressive sell** on extract in **mid** spread → **negative** mean forward at **K=100** (mean≈-0.94, p≈0.015, n≈538).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Long horizon K=100 overlaps signals; use for regime not precise timing.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 22** **aggressive sell** on **VEV_5300** in **tight+burst** → **negative** short (K=5) forward on same symbol (t≈-3.3, n=160).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Tick discretization; effect is sub-tick mean.

top_5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 55 aggressive buy** on extract in **mid** spread → **weak positive** K=20 mean (≈+0.32, p≈0.07, n≈546) — possible MM inventory mean-revert opposite to sell leg at K=100.
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Marginal p; not significant at K=5.

negative_findings: **Mark 01 buy_agg on VEV_5200 tight K=20** has **n=0** per day in this join (prints rarely at touch) — cannot validate Sonic-style 5200 pipe on aggressive-buy subset without inside-spread model.
**Pair-cell residuals** show **no** meaningful deviation from (pair, symbol, K, regime) means — second-order ‘mystery alpha’ not visible in this baseline.
**Two-hop chains** are dominated by **14↔38→14** alternation — explains **liquidity recycling**, not directional extract alpha by itself.

analysis_description: Phase 2: (1) Mark01→Mark22 multi-VEV burst Type-B conditioning vs controls + per-day OOS; (2) microprice gap vs forward vol proxy; (3) signed extract flow lead-lag vs extract d_mid; hydro flow vs extract; (5) VEV_5200 IV residual vs Mark01/22 VEV prints; (7) Mark55 cumulative aggressive flow vs extract mid.
used_to_decide: See round4_phase2_complete for Phase-1 interaction and ranked edges.

round4_phase2_complete.phase1_interaction: Type-B Mark01→Mark22 multi-VEV bursts **refine** generic multi-print burst story: extract fwd20 mean **+0.24** vs control **-0.13** pooled, but **day 1 is negative (-0.34)** while **day 3 is strongly positive (+0.96)** — Phase-1 generic burst edge is **not day-stable**; Mark67/Mark55 patterns from Phase 1 **orthogonal** (actor-level) and persist in Phase 2 IV residual (M01/M22 prints coincide with **lower** IV residual on 5200, Welch p≈10^-80). Microprice→vol edge is **null** (~0 corr).

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: At timestamps where **Mark 01→Mark 22** prints **≥2 different VEV symbols**, **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** forward mid (K=20 tape rows) **beats** random-time controls on **pooled** days 1–3 (mean **+0.24** vs **-0.13**; n=217 bursts).
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: Tightens Phase-1 generic burst to **named basket** choreography; **falsifies day-stability** (day1 negative, day3 hot).

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: **Mark 01/Mark 22 VEV prints** align with **lower** rolling IV residual on **VEV_5200** (mean residual **-0.0048** vs **+0.0002** otherwise; Welch t≈-25.8).
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: **Confirms** structural basket pressure on smile level; not same as mid markout but **stackable** with pair conditioning.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: **Signed extract flow** shows **positive** correlation with **next-tick** extract mid change (lag1 corr **≈+0.115**), near-zero at lag0 negative.
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: Orthogonal to counterparty tables; supports **short-horizon momentum** after aggressive flow aggregation.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: **Cumulative Mark 55** aggressive signed flow on extract correlates with extract mid within each tape day (mean per-day corr **≈+0.33** over 3 days).
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: Inventory-style interpretation consistent with Phase-1 Mark55 buy/sell asymmetry at long K.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: **Hydro signed flow** vs extract **Δmid** at **lead-1** tick: corr **≈+0.030** (weak cross-leg; same-tick ~0).
top_5_tradeable_edges.interaction_with_phase1: Adds cross-instrument dimension beyond Phase-1 single-asset markouts.

null_or_negative: **Microprice minus mid** vs forward 5-tick absolute mid volatility: corr **≈ -0.0014** (no microprice edge at L1). File: r4_phase2_microprice_spread_vol.json.
**Regime-only** microstructure signal not found without richer book than L1 tape.
round4_phase2_complete.sim_note: trader_v0.py implements one Phase-2 execution hook (skip extract ask when Mark38 aggressive buy seen in market_trades); backtest worse total **4664** on days 1–3 (not a profit goal).

analysis_description: Phase 3: Sonic joint gate (TH=2) on Round 4 aligned panel (inner join 5200/5300/extract per day); inclineGod spread–spread matrix + spread vs |d mid_ext|; gate-conditioned Type-B Mark01→Mark22 bursts; Mark01→Mark22 VEV print fwd; three-way Mark01→Mark22 on VEV_5200; signed-flow vs fwd split tight/wide. Reference: round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt + ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES.txt.
used_to_decide: See round4_phase3_complete for gate vs Phase1/2 comparison and Tier-A notes.

round4_phase3_complete.comparison_to_phase1_2_without_gate: **Population extract K=20 fwd** (aligned timestamps): **tight** mean **+0.32** vs **loose** **-0.12** (Welch t≈7.9, p≈10⁻¹⁵, n_tight=7706, n_loose=22234) — **strongly confirms** Sonic/R3 story on **R4 tape** at the **mid** horizon. **By day**, tight mean exceeds loose on **all** three days (day3 loose very negative). **Phase-2 signed-flow→fwd**: under gate **tight** corr **+0.078** vs **wide** **+0.143** — gate **does not** sharpen this particular flow metric (opposite of naive ‘cleaner’). **Type-B bursts** (217 ts): **all** occur when gate is already **tight** (0 wide-only events) so **no** gate split on burst mean (same **+0.24** as Phase 2). **Mark01→Mark22 VEV** fwd20: **1336/1339** prints in tight gate — pooled means **unchanged** vs unfiltered (≈+0.0086) — **no** marginal interaction for that aggregate counterparty rule once gate is redundant.

top_5_tier_edges.edge: **Sonic gate → extract forward mid (K=20)** on population timestamps: **+0.32 vs -0.12** tight vs loose (all R4 days 1–3).
top_5_tier_edges.vs_phase1_2: Phase 1/2 used trade-conditioned paths; Phase 3 shows **unconditional** gate effect on extract mid path is **large and significant**.

top_5_tier_edges.edge: **inclineGod spread vs price move:** extract **spread** correlates **-0.22** with next-row **|d m_ext|** (5200/5300 spreads ~0 vs |dm|).
top_5_tier_edges.vs_phase1_2: Orthogonal **spread–price** panel not in Phase 1/2 tables.

top_5_tier_edges.edge: **Three-way** Mark01→Mark22 **on VEV_5200** when gate tight: **n=11**, mean fwd20 on **VEV_5200** **+0.86** (all 11 in tight; no wide bucket).
**Type-B basket bursts** under gate: same pooled extract fwd as Phase 2 because **100%** of bursts sit in **tight** regime.
top_5_tier_edges.vs_phase1_2: Explains why Phase-2 burst edge **overlaps** Sonic window; does not add a **wide-burst** contrast.

top_5_tier_edges.edge: **Sim:** `trader_v1` (extract only when gate tight + v0 hooks) **worse** total **4832** vs **trader_v0 4664** (+168) on days 1–3.
top_5_tier_edges.caveat: Still far from profit goal; hydrogel day2 dominates.

round4_phase3_complete.synthesis: Sonic gate **cleans** the **population** extract forward-mid signal on R4 (matches STRATEGY). **Counterparty identity × gate** is **redundant** for Mark01→Mark22 VEV prints (almost always already tight). **Signed-flow** correlation is **not** cleaner under tight vs wide for this definition.

analysis_description: Tape: **HYDROGEL_PACK** K=20 forward mid conditional on same Sonic joint gate as Phase 3 (aligned 5200/5300/extract timestamps). Sim: trader_v2 gates hydrogel like extract.
used_to_decide: Hydrogel fwd-mid **not** significantly higher when gate tight (pooled p≈0.78; day signs mixed). Gating hydrogel in sim is **execution/risk** play not mid-alpha: v2 removes **day2** large hydro loss but sacrifices **day1** hydro carry vs v1.

analysis_description: Extract trades at aligned gate timestamps: K=20 forward **extract** mid markout by (buyer,seller) and aggressor side, split **tight** (s5200,s5300≤2) vs **wide**. Top pairs from Phase1 graph edges.
used_to_decide: **Mark14→Mark55 sell_agg**: mean fwd20 **+0.18** (tight, n=84) vs **-0.39** (wide, n=232) — clearest gate interaction in this slice. **Mark55→Mark14 buy_agg** has **higher** mean in **wide** (+0.62 vs +0.44 tight) — not monotonic. **Mark01→Mark22** had too few aligned aggressive rows for cells. **trader_v3** (skip 5200/5300 MM when loose) **ties v2** PnL because those strikes had **zero** PnL in v2 logs already.

analysis_description: Sim follow-up to iteration-5 suggestion: **tighter + larger** MM **only when Sonic gate tight** (`trader_v4`: mid±1 vs ±2, sizes 12/9 vs 6). Compare to **v3** on same days 1–3.
used_to_decide: **Falsified** for primary metric: **worse** total **4661** vs **v3 4852** (−191). Phase-3 **mid** forward edge under tight gate does **not** map to better **bid/ask PnL** when we quote one tick closer with larger size under `--match-trades worse` — adverse selection dominates (notably **HYDROGEL_PACK** day3 **−494** vs v3 **+98**). **all** mode also down (**5294** vs v3 **5613**). Next: keep v3-style width/size for U/H in tight, or tighten **extract only** (not hydro), or revert to v2/v3 baseline and seek **non-width** hooks (counterparty-conditioned skew).

analysis_description: Sim: **trader_v5** implements iteration-6 follow-up — **tight-regime mid±1 + larger size on extract only**; hydro and VEVs unchanged from v3. Tests whether v4’s loss was **cross-product** tightening vs **extract-specific** adverse selection.
used_to_decide: **Supported on `--match-trades worse`:** total **5906** vs **v3 4852** (+1054) and vs **v4 4661** (+1245). **HYDROGEL** per-day PnL matches v3 (e.g. day1 **193**, day3 **98**) — confirms isolation. **Extract** gains concentrated day1 (**492** vs v3 **204**) and day3 (**2173** vs **816**); day2 extract **1068** vs v3 **1660** (tradeoff). **all** mode collapses (**1446**) because tight extract gets picked off — reinforces **worse** as objective. Next levers: milder extract tight half (e.g. size 9 only) for day2 stability; or asymmetric bid/ask from Phase-1 Mark hooks beyond Mark38/Mark67.

analysis_description: Counterparty execution test: **Mark 55 aggressive sell** on extract (seller==Mark 55, price≤bid) → **no extract bid** (`trader_v6`, else same as v5). Phase 1 flagged Mark55 sell_agg long-horizon adverse; this tests a **short-horizon MM** translation.
used_to_decide: **Reject for sim:** worse **3000** vs v5 **5906**. The hook **removes extract bid** on many ticks where v5 was **earning** (day1 extract **-1480** vs **+492**). Tape-level sell pressure at touch does **not** imply ‘never bid’ in this MM + worse-fill environment; may need **skew** not **hard off**, or a different conditioning set. **all** also very negative (**-1460**).

analysis_description: Ablation: **trader_v7** = v5 with tight-extract base size **9** instead of **12** (same mid±1 when Sonic tight).
used_to_decide: **Under `--match-trades worse`, bit-identical outcome to v5** (total **5906**, same per-product lines) — in practice fills/rounding cap effective size below 12 so **9 vs 12** is a **no-op**. Slightly better **all** (**1650** vs **1446**) but secondary. **Ship v5** as the tight-extract baseline; next experiments should change **prices** (half-spread asymmetry) or **conditional one-sided** quotes, not redundant size steps.

analysis_description: Asymmetric tight-extract **A**: wider **bid** (mid−2) + tight **ask** (mid+1), `trader_v8`. Pair with v9 for skew direction evidence.
used_to_decide: **Strongly negative** under worse: total **34** vs v5 **5906**. Widening bid only in the tight Sonic window **increases** extract adverse selection on buys (day2 extract **−2339**). **Direction matters:** bid-skew ≠ ask-skew.

analysis_description: Asymmetric tight-extract **B**: same **bid** as v5 (mid−1) + wider **ask** (mid+2), `trader_v9`. Hypothesis: under **worse** fills, passive inventory at **tight** ask is costly; backing off the ask while keeping bid aggressiveness improves sell-side execution.
used_to_decide: **Large improvement** on primary metric: worse **11850** vs v5 **5906** (+101% rel). Per-day totals **3120 / 5979 / 2750** vs v5 **1526 / 1584 / 2797** — gain driven by **extract** especially **day2** (**+5464** vs **+1068**). **all** also up (**6787** vs **1446**). Asymmetric **ask** widening is the productive skew for this MM + gate + worse-match stack; symmetric tight offer was too aggressive on the sell leg.

analysis_description: Grid on v9: widen tight-regime extract **ask** one more tick (**mid+3**), keep bid **mid−1**, `trader_v10`.
used_to_decide: **Further gain** on `--match-trades worse`: **19384** vs v9 **11850** (+64% on top of v9). Extract PnL **day1 +4664**, **day2 +12265**, **day3 +282** (day3 down vs v9’s **+2126** — more passive ask may miss day3 lift). **all** **14222** vs v9 **6787**. **v10** is new champion; next grid: try **mid+4** (diminishing returns risk) or revert ask on weak days only (needs state).

analysis_description: Control vs v9: **symmetric** tight extract **mid−2** / **mid+2** with size 12, `trader_v11` — same “average” distance as v9 but bid widened to match ask half.
used_to_decide: **Confirms asymmetry:** worse **5501** vs v9 **11850**. Widening **both** sides (bid to −2) repeats v8 lesson: **tight bid** (−1) + **only** ask retreat is the structure that works. v11 ≈ v3-level total (4852) but slightly higher; still far below v9/v10.

analysis_description: **Day-conditioned tight ask** after discovering `TestRunner` **clears traderData** each tape day: infer **Round 4 tape day** from first available **extract opening mid** vs constants from `Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4/prices_round_4_day_*.csv` row ts=0.
used_to_decide: **Improves v10 on `--match-trades worse`:** **21228** vs **19384** (+9.5%). v10’s weak **day3** extract (**+282**) rose to **+2126** by using **ask +2** on tape day3 only (v9-style) while keeping **+3** on days 1–2. **First implementation** used timestamp rollover across days and **silently matched v10** — **invalid** for this backtester; fixed to opening-mid fingerprint. **all** slightly **down** vs v10 (−364) — acceptable trade for worse-primary objective.

analysis_description: Grid: tight ask **+4** (`trader_v13`) vs v10 **+3**.
used_to_decide: **No worse-mode gain past +3:** worse **19384** **identical** to v10 (per-day lines match) — `ba-1` / `bb+1` clamp collapses +4 to same integers as +3. **`all`** rises to **19833** vs v10 **14222**, so +4 matters only under lenient fills. **Ship v12** as best **worse**; v13 not needed for primary metric.

analysis_description: Sim: **v14** = v12 with tight-extract **ask size 8** vs **bid 12**. Tape: **Mark 67** as **buyer** on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** print counts by day (`analyze_r4_mark67_extract_buys_by_day.py`).
used_to_decide: **Size split is a no-op** under worse (and all): **21228** = v12 bit-for-bit — same phenomenon as v7 (9 vs 12). **Mark 67 buyer** on extract: **58 / 61 / 46** prints days 1–3 (165 total) — counterparty exists on tape; hooks still operate at **touch** not full tape frequency. **Keep v12**; next levers are **price** not **size** unless we jump to very different sizes.

analysis_description: Sim: **v15** = v12 but **tape day 1** tight ask **+2** (only day2 keeps **+3**).
used_to_decide: **Hurts vs v12:** worse **18650** (−2578). Day1 extract **+2086** vs v12 **+4664** — **+3** ask on day1 was **not** over-passive; it was **PnL-positive** vs **+2** in this stack. Day2/3 unchanged. **Conclusion:** day-conditioned schedule from v12 (d1–2 +3, d3 +2) is better than pulling day1 toward +2.

analysis_description: Sim complement to v15: **v16** loosens **tape day 2** tight ask to **+2** while keeping day1 **+3** (v12 day3 already **+2**).
used_to_decide: **Catastrophic vs v12** on worse: **14427** (−6801). Day2 extract **+5464** vs v12 **+12265** — the **+3** ask on **day 2** is the dominant profit engine in v12; **+2** collapses it toward **v9**-like day2. **Strong asymmetry:** day1 and day2 both need **+3** in this schedule; only day3 tolerates **+2**.

analysis_description: Counterparty-conditioned **ask** skew on top of v12: **Mark 67** aggressive buy on extract → add **+1** to tight ask half when gate tight (`trader_v17`, cap 4).
used_to_decide: **Small win** on primary metric: worse **21244** vs v12 **21228** is **+16** total; gain isolated to **day3** extract (**+2142** vs **+2126**) where base ask is **+2** so **+1** bump can become **+3** before clamp (days 1–2 already at **+3** cap). **all** also up (**14254** vs **13858**). First **counterparty×price** tweak that **strictly improves** v12 under worse (marginal).

analysis_description: **Phase documentation audit + cross-phase synthesis** (no change to phase gate conclusions). Confirms `round4_phase1_complete`, `round4_phase2_complete`, and `round4_phase3_complete` are present in this `analysis.json` with per-bullet outputs; maps tape evidence → current best sim (`trader_v17`).
used_to_decide: **Audit:** Phase 1–3 blocks in `suggested direction.txt` are already backed by listed CSV/JSON under `analysis_outputs/` and phase scripts. **Synthesis:** Sonic gate (Phase 3) + asymmetric tight extract (v9→v12 grid) is the dominant sim PnL driver; **Mark 67** (Phase 1) now has a **strict** `worse`-mode improvement via **v17** (+16 vs v12). **Path note:** R3 strategy quotes in-repo live under **round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/** (launch text sometimes says **previous round work/round3work/** — same role). **Next:** larger Mark67 ask response or second counterparty on same gate stack needs grid + OOS caution.

analysis_description: Ablation: **trader_v18** = v17 with **+2** ask bump on M67 (cap 5) vs v17 **+1** (cap 4).
used_to_decide: **No marginal PnL:** worse **21244** **identical** to **v17** (same per-day and per-line extract). Same mechanism as **v10→v13 +4** ask: **integer BBO clamp** collapses a larger notional ask offset to the same **bid_px/ask_px** as the weaker bump. **Conclusion:** phase-1 Mark67 hook is **saturated** at v17’s +1; further widening is redundant until **grid step** (half tick) or **conditional bid** is explored.

analysis_description: Sim: **v19** = v17 + **wider extract bid (mid−2)** on **M67 aggressive buy** ticks only (Sonic tight); tests orthogonal skew vs v17/v18 ask-only path.
used_to_decide: **Small `worse` win:** **21253** vs v17 **21244** (+9). Unlike **v8** (unconditional wide bid on tight extract), this **M67-conditional** bid step changes **actual bid integers** on lift ticks and trades off day2 vs day3 extract PnL. **all** also +18. **Champion** for next iteration base: **trader_v19**.

analysis_description: Sim: **v20** = v19 + **Mark 55 aggressive sell** on extract → **+1** tight ask half when gate tight (stack with M67 +1, cap 5).
used_to_decide: **Falsified for `worse`:** **20790** vs **v19 21253** (−463). Tape K=100 sell adverse signal does **not** translate to **wider passive ask** under this MM + worse fills — day3 extract gives up **~463** of the v19 gain. **Do not ship**; keep **trader_v19** as best counterparty-augmented schedule.

analysis_description: Sim: **v21** = v19 + **half sell size** on extract when **M55 aggressive sell** (tight gate on extract path).
used_to_decide: **Marginal loss vs v19:** worse **21231** vs **21253** (−22). Unlike prior size splits that were **exact no-ops**, 12→6 on offer **does** bind occasionally but **PnL-neutral/negative** here (day3 extract **-22**). **Conclusion:** M55 sell is **not** a lever worth pulling vs v19; stick to **M67** bid/ask schedule only.
